title: Bridge Paper: Genome-wide association study identifies genetic loci for self-reported habitual sleep duration supported by accelerometer-derived estimates.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mechanicality** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Habit — automaticity, habitual behavior, action monitoring, loss of conscious control.
**Phase context:** habit, automaticity, habitual behavior, action monitoring, chunked behavior

Paper: Genome-wide association study identifies genetic loci for self-reported habitual sleep duration supported by accelerometer-derived estimates.
DOI: 10.1038/s41467-019-08917-4
Abstract: Sleep is an essential state of decreased activity and alertness but molecular factors regulating sleep duration remain unknown. Through genome-wide association analysis in 446,118 adults of European ancestry from the UK Biobank, we identify 78 loci for self-reported habitual sleep duration (p < 5 × 10-8; 43 loci at p < 6 × 10-9). Replication is observed for PAX8, VRK2, and FBXL12/UBL5/PIN1 loci in the CHARGE study (n = 47,180; p < 6.3 × 10-4), and 55 signals show sign-concordant effects. The 78
